name: Mickey Rooney
abstract: Mickey Rooney (born Ninnian Joseph Yule Jr.; September 23, 1920 – April 6, 2014) was an American actor. In a career spanning nearly nine decades, he appeared in more than 300 films and was among the last surviving stars of the silent-film era.

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: Watched Mickey Rooney and Andy Hardy. If they could do it, why couldn't we do it?
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: He look like he about four feet...he's about the size of Mickey Rooney.

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Collette
about_quotes.evidence: One of the comedians was Yule, Joe Yule, Mickey Rooney’s father.
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Collette interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gallodoro
about_quotes.evidence: He took four musicians. Trumpet, banjo, myself, and... What was the fourth one? To do the strike up the band picture with Mickey Rooney and Judy Garland. ... We had a few shots in it.

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t think many of the young people or older people may not remember the name Joe Pasternak. He was the biggest producer of musicals. Judy Garland and Mickey Rooney.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: Then I did The Strip with Mickey Rooney, which I wasn't photographed in it, I did the soundtrack and Mickey Rooney did the synching of it, synchronizing.
